response:
AC-001

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is less than or equal to ₹1,00,000
When the customer initiates a transfer from the source account to an own destination account with the transfer amount
Then the source account balance is debited by the transfer amount
And the destination account balance is credited by the transfer amount.

AC-002

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is less than or equal to ₹1,00,000
When the customer initiates a transfer from the source account to an own destination account with the transfer amount
Then a transaction reference number is generated.

AC-003

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is less than or equal to ₹1,00,000
When the customer initiates a transfer from the source account to an own destination account with the transfer amount
Then the transaction is recorded for audit.

AC-004

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with an insufficient balance
When the customer initiates a transfer from the source account to an own destination account with a transfer amount
Then the transfer fails.

AC-005

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with an insufficient balance
When the customer initiates a transfer from the source account to an own destination account with a transfer amount
Then the source account balance remains unchanged
And the destination account balance remains unchanged.

AC-006

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with an insufficient balance
When the customer initiates a transfer from the source account to an own destination account with a transfer amount
Then the failed transaction is recorded for audit.

AC-007

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is greater than ₹1,00,000
When the customer initiates a transfer from the source account to an own destination account with the transfer amount
Then the system prompts for OTP verification.

AC-008

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is greater than ₹1,00,000
And a valid OTP is provided
When the customer completes the OTP verification for the transfer
Then the source account balance is debited by the transfer amount
And the destination account balance is credited by the transfer amount.

AC-009

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is greater than ₹1,00,000
And a valid OTP is provided
When the customer completes the OTP verification for the transfer
Then a transaction reference number is generated.

AC-010

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is greater than ₹1,00,000
And a valid OTP is provided
When the customer completes the OTP verification for the transfer
Then the transaction is recorded for audit.

AC-011

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is greater than ₹1,00,000
And an invalid OTP is provided
When the customer attempts to complete the OTP verification for the transfer
Then the transfer fails.

AC-012

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is greater than ₹1,00,000
And an invalid OTP is provided
When the customer attempts to complete the OTP verification for the transfer
Then the source account balance remains unchanged
And the destination account balance remains unchanged.

AC-013

Given an authenticated customer has a source account with a sufficient balance
And the transfer amount is greater than ₹1,00,000
And an invalid OTP is provided
When the customer attempts to complete the OTP verification for the transfer
Then the failed transaction is recorded for audit.
